title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Tuckahoe, NY | $900–$2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Tuckahoe, Westchester. Humane bat eviction, attic cleanup, and entry sealing. Licensed experts serving Tuckahoe, NY. Call today!
intro: Dealing with bats in your Tuckahoe home or business? Best Pest Control NYC provides comprehensive bat removal and exclusion services throughout Tuckahoe and the surrounding Westchester area. Our licensed technicians use humane, effective methods to safely remove bats and prevent their return, protecting your property and family from health risks.
whyUs: With over a decade of experience serving Tuckahoe and Westchester County, Best Pest Control NYC combines local expertise with proven bat exclusion techniques. We offer thorough inspections, humane removal, complete attic sanitization, and guaranteed exclusion work to ensure bats don't return to your property.
localContext: Tuckahoe's charming historic homes and tree-lined streets near the Bronx River Parkway create ideal conditions for bat colonies to roost in attics, chimneys, and soffits. From Main Street's commercial buildings to the residential neighborhoods surrounding Parkway Oval, our team understands the unique structural challenges of Tuckahoe properties when it comes to effective bat exclusion.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Tuckahoe?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Tuckahoe typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, extent of attic damage, and the complexity of your property's structure. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before any work begins.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in Westchester County?
faqs.answer: Yes, but bat removal in New York must follow specific regulations. Bats cannot be removed during maternity season (roughly May through August) when flightless pups are present. Our licensed professionals ensure all removal and exclusion work complies with New York State wildlife laws and uses only humane exclusion methods.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have bats in my Tuckahoe home?
faqs.answer: Common signs of bat infestation include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, dark grease stains around potential entry points, accumulation of bat droppings (guano) in attics or near entry points, and seeing bats flying around your home at twilight. If you notice any of these signs, contact us for a professional inspection.

faqs.question: What does the bat exclusion process involve?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive bat exclusion process includes a detailed inspection to locate all entry points and roosting areas, installation of one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to leave but not return, sealing all potential entry points once bats have vacated, attic cleanup and sanitization to remove guano and contamination, and a warranty to guarantee bats won't return.
